title: trifolium pratense var. nivale
intro: Trifolium pratense var. nivale, also known as white clover, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the Fabaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in isoflavones, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, providing various health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, immune system support, and reduced risk of certain diseases. Trifolium pratense var. nivale can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as dried and used in soups and other culinary dishes.
